questions.question: A student argues that curd formation and antibiotic production show the same role of microbes because both are useful to humans. Which distinction is most useful for evaluating this claim?
options: Curd formation uses microbial metabolic activity in food, while antibiotic production uses microbial products against other microbes.
Curd formation depends on viruses, while antibiotic production depends only on bacteria used in food.
Both processes are identical because microbes only convert milk sugar into acids.
Antibiotic production is a household process, while curd formation is mainly an industrial process.

questions.question: Statement I: Some microbes are useful in sewage treatment because they help reduce organic matter. Statement II: The same role proves that all microbes in sewage are beneficial. Which option best evaluates the statements?
options: Statement I is correct, but Statement II overgeneralises the useful role of selected microbial communities.
Both statements are correct because sewage treatment depends on every microbe present in sewage.
Statement I is incorrect because organic matter is removed only by mechanical filtration.
Statement II is correct because harmful microbes are removed before treatment begins.
